g.text: Buffer Resize via ioctl: Edge Cases and Data Integrity (mydevice.c)
new_buf | char* | kfree'd by kernel (NULL returned)
Race Window WITHOUT mutex (M3 has no lock)
Step A | CPU0 in resize: new_buf allocated, old not freed yet
Step B | CPU1 in read: reads kernel_buffer (old pointer, valid)
Step C | CPU0 swaps: kfree(old), kernel_buffer = new_buf
Step D | CPU1 continues: kernel_buffer now freed — USE-AFTER-FREE
Red path = error return, original buffer unchanged
Green path = grow: all data preserved
Orange path= shrink-truncate: data loss, returns 0
Purple box = concurrency hazard fixed in M4
decision | refuse: data would not fit
swap is NOT atomic in M3
